revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]Created page with '{{{eq|{{{1}}}}}} ---- This template prevents line breaks during HTML mathematics. It is a practical alternativ...'
revision.text:
{{{eq|{{{1}}}}}} ---- This template prevents line breaks during HTML mathematics. It is a practical alternative to excessive use of [[non-breaking space]]s ( ). ==Use== In a math article, included math such as 
''f'': ''X'' × ''Y'' → [0, 1]
may end up broken between lines. This can be fixed using nonbreaking spaces: 
''f'': ''X'' × ''Y'' → [0, 1]
However, this results in cumbersome source code which can be difficult to edit. Using this template, the same result can be achieved with: 
{{mbox| ''f'': ''X'' × ''Y'' → [0, 1] }}
===Dealing with equations=== Because of Wikipedia limitations on templates, mbox doesn't function as stated above if the math contains an equals sign. In this case, the following syntax should be used: 
{{mbox|eq= 2''x'' + 3''y'' + 5''z'' = 7 }}

revision.comment: /* Dealing with equations */
revision.text:
{{{eq|{{{1}}}}}} ---- This template prevents line breaks during HTML mathematics. It is a practical alternative to excessive use of [[non-breaking space]]s ( ). ==Use== In a math article, included math such as 
''f'': ''X'' × ''Y'' → [0, 1]
may end up broken between lines. This can be fixed using nonbreaking spaces: 
''f'': ''X'' × ''Y'' → [0, 1]
However, this results in cumbersome source code which can be difficult to edit. Using this template, the same result can be achieved with: 
{{mbox| ''f'': ''X'' × ''Y'' → [0, 1] }}
===Dealing with equations=== Because of Wikipedia limitations on templates, mbox doesn't function as stated above if the math contains an equals sign. In this case, the following syntax should be used: 
{{mbox|eq= 2''x'' + 3''y'' + 5''z'' = 7 }}
This longer syntax also works for non-equations.

===Dealing with equations=== Because of Wikipedia limitations on templates, mbox doesn't function as stated above if the math contains an equals sign. In this case, the following syntax should be used: 
{{mbox|eq= 2''x'' + 3''y'' + 5''z'' = 7}}
This longer syntax also works for non-equations.

{{{|{{{1}}}}}} ---- This template prevents line breaks during HTML mathematics. It is a practical alternative to excessive use of [[non-breaking space]]s ( ). ==Use== In a math article, included math such as

{{mbox|= 2''x'' + 3''y'' + 5''z'' = 7}}
Using |= instead of | also works for non-equations. [[Category:Mathematics templates|{{PAGENAME}}]]

revision.comment: steal unused template for a new high-level meta-template (for interaction between {{ambox}}, {{imbox}} and {{cmbox}}
more rigorous
add {{dmbox}}
add doc
Changed the "#ifeq:" statements to "#if:", changed to using {{namespace detect}} so a demospace parameter can be used and talk space can be easily detected, and other fixes.
Protected Template:Mbox: High-risk template (soon to be, anyway :D ) [edit=sysop:move=sysop]
template moved
Updating the code comment since ambox and dmbox/ombox has been fixed. They can take empty parameters now.
A first beta version of {{tmbox}} is available, so enabled call to tmbox.
The if-statements do not work, have done extensive testing in my userspace. But no worries, all our mboxes now can take empty parameters so lets use that.
Adding "{{pp-template|small=yes}}".
Adding support for the {{tmbox}} small parameters.
Changed "text = {{{text|}}}" to "text = {{{text}}}" so that the empty box displays "{{{text}}}".
Changed deprecated "{{namespace detect|image=}}" to "{{namespace detect|file=}}, due to change of namespace "Image:" to "File:". No change in functionality.
This template has now been checked and tested, it works correctly in the new "Book:" and "Book talk:" namespaces. And a minor update to "{{pp-template}}".
redundant
pass subst and date for benefit of ambox
pass through class
pass name for benefit of ambox
switch to Lua version

{{{eq|{{{1}}}}}} ---- This template prevents line breaks during HTML mathematics. It is a practical alternative to excessive use of [[non-breaking space]]s ( ). ==Use== In a math article, included math such as 
''f'': ''X'' × ''Y'' → [0, 1]
may end up broken between lines. This can be fixed using nonbreaking spaces: 
''f'': ''X'' × ''Y'' → [0, 1]
However, this results in cumbersome source code which can be difficult to edit. Using this template, the same result can be achieved with: 
{{mbox| ''f'': ''X'' × ''Y'' → [0, 1] }}
===Dealing with equations=== Because of Wikipedia limitations on templates, mbox doesn't function as stated above if the math contains an equals sign. In this case, the following syntax should be used: 
{{mbox|eq= 2''x'' + 3''y'' + 5''z'' = 7 }}

{{mbox| ''f'': ''X'' × ''Y'' → [0, 1]}}
===Dealing with equations=== Because of Wikipedia limitations on templates, mbox doesn't function as stated above if the math contains an equals sign. In this case, the following syntax should be used: 
{{mbox|= 2''x'' + 3''y'' + 5''z'' = 7}}
Using |= instead of | also works for non-equations.

''f'': ''X'' × ''Y'' → [0, 1]
may end up broken between lines. This can be fixed using nonbreaking spaces: 
''f'': ''X'' × ''Y'' → [0, 1]
However, this results in cumbersome source code which can be difficult to edit. Using this template, the same result can be achieved with: 
{{mbox| ''f'': ''X'' × ''Y'' → [0, 1]}}
===Dealing with equations=== Because of Wikipedia limitations on templates, mbox doesn't function as stated above if the math contains an equals sign. In this case, the following syntax should be used: 
{{mbox|= 2''x'' + 3''y'' + 5''z'' = 7}}
Using |= instead of | also works for non-equations. [[Category:Mathematics templates|{{PAGENAME}}]]

revision.text:
{{{eq|{{{1}}}}}} ---- This template prevents line breaks during HTML mathematics. It is a practical alternative to excessive use of [[non-breaking space]]s ( ). ==Use== In a math article, included math such as 
''f'': ''X'' × ''Y'' → [0, 1]
may end up broken between lines. This can be fixed using nonbreaking spaces: 
''f'': ''X'' × ''Y'' → [0, 1]
However, this results in cumbersome source code which can be difficult to edit. Using this template, the same result can be achieved with: 
{{mbox| ''f'': ''X'' × ''Y'' → [0, 1] }}
===Dealing with equations=== Because of Wikipedia limitations on templates, mbox doesn't function as stated above if the math contains an equals sign. In this case, the following syntax should be used: 
{{mbox|eq= 2''x'' + 3''y'' + 5''z'' = 7 }}

{{mbox|eq= 2''x'' + 3''y'' + 5''z'' = 7 }}

revision.comment: /* Dealing with equations */
revision.text:
{{{eq|{{{1}}}}}} ---- This template prevents line breaks during HTML mathematics. It is a practical alternative to excessive use of [[non-breaking space]]s ( ). ==Use== In a math article, included math such as 
''f'': ''X'' × ''Y'' → [0, 1]
may end up broken between lines. This can be fixed using nonbreaking spaces: 
''f'': ''X'' × ''Y'' → [0, 1]
However, this results in cumbersome source code which can be difficult to edit. Using this template, the same result can be achieved with: 
{{mbox| ''f'': ''X'' × ''Y'' → [0, 1] }}

{{mbox|eq= 2''x'' + 3''y'' + 5''z'' = 7 }}
This longer syntax also works for non-equations.

revision.comment: steal unused template for a new high-level meta-template (for interaction between {{ambox}}, {{imbox}} and {{cmbox}}
more rigorous
add {{dmbox}}
add doc
Changed the "#ifeq:" statements to "#if:", changed to using {{namespace detect}} so a demospace parameter can be used and talk space can be easily detected, and other fixes.
template moved
Updating the code comment since ambox and dmbox/ombox has been fixed. They can take empty parameters now.
A first beta version of {{tmbox}} is available, so enabled call to tmbox.
The if-statements do not work, have done extensive testing in my userspace. But no worries, all our mboxes now can take empty parameters so lets use that.
Adding "{{pp-template|small=yes}}".
Adding support for the {{tmbox}} small parameters.
Changed "text = {{{text|}}}" to "text = {{{text}}}" so that the empty box displays "{{{text}}}".
Changed deprecated "{{namespace detect|image=}}" to "{{namespace detect|file=}}, due to change of namespace "Image:" to "File:". No change in functionality.
This template has now been checked and tested, it works correctly in the new "Book:" and "Book talk:" namespaces. And a minor update to "{{pp-template}}".
redundant
pass subst and date for benefit of ambox
pass through class
pass name for benefit of ambox
switch to Lua version
revision.text: {{#invoke:Message box|mbox}}<noinclude> {{documentation}} <!-- Categories go on the /doc subpage, and interwikis go on Wikidata. --> </noinclude>

revision.comment: /* Dealing with equations */
revision.text:
{{{|{{{1}}}}}} ---- This template prevents line breaks during HTML mathematics. It is a practical alternative to excessive use of [[non-breaking space]]s ( ). ==Use== In a math article, included math such as 
''f'': ''X'' × ''Y'' → [0, 1]
may end up broken between lines. This can be fixed using nonbreaking spaces: 
''f'': ''X'' × ''Y'' → [0, 1]
However, this results in cumbersome source code which can be difficult to edit. Using this template, the same result can be achieved with: 
{{mbox| ''f'': ''X'' × ''Y'' → [0, 1]}}
===Dealing with equations=== Because of Wikipedia limitations on templates, mbox doesn't function as stated above if the math contains an equals sign. In this case, the following syntax should be used: 
{{mbox|= 2''x'' + 3''y'' + 5''z'' = 7}}
Using |= instead of | also works for non-equations.

{{{|{{{1}}}}}} ---- This template prevents line breaks during HTML mathematics. It is a practical alternative to excessive use of [[non-breaking space]]s ( ). ==Use== In a math article, included math such as 
''f'': ''X'' × ''Y'' → [0, 1]
may end up broken between lines. This can be fixed using nonbreaking spaces: 
''f'': ''X'' × ''Y'' → [0, 1]
However, this results in cumbersome source code which can be difficult to edit. Using this template, the same result can be achieved with: 
{{mbox| ''f'': ''X'' × ''Y'' → [0, 1]}}
===Dealing with equations=== Because of Wikipedia limitations on templates, mbox doesn't function as stated above if the math contains an equals sign. In this case, the following syntax should be used: 
{{mbox|= 2''x'' + 3''y'' + 5''z'' = 7}}
Using |= instead of | also works for non-equations. [[Category:Mathematics templates|{{PAGENAME}}]]

revision.comment: steal unused template for a new high-level meta-template (for interaction between {{ambox}}, {{imbox}} and {{cmbox}}
more rigorous
add {{dmbox}}
add doc
Changed the "#ifeq:" statements to "#if:", changed to using {{namespace detect}} so a demospace parameter can be used and talk space can be easily detected, and other fixes.
template moved
Updating the code comment since ambox and dmbox/ombox has been fixed. They can take empty parameters now.
A first beta version of {{tmbox}} is available, so enabled call to tmbox.
The if-statements do not work, have done extensive testing in my userspace. But no worries, all our mboxes now can take empty parameters so lets use that.
Adding "{{pp-template|small=yes}}".
Adding support for the {{tmbox}} small parameters.
Changed "text = {{{text|}}}" to "text = {{{text}}}" so that the empty box displays "{{{text}}}".
Changed deprecated "{{namespace detect|image=}}" to "{{namespace detect|file=}}, due to change of namespace "Image:" to "File:". No change in functionality.
This template has now been checked and tested, it works correctly in the new "Book:" and "Book talk:" namespaces. And a minor update to "{{pp-template}}".
redundant
pass subst and date for benefit of ambox
pass through class
pass name for benefit of ambox
switch to Lua version
revision.text: {{#invoke:Message box|mbox}}<noinclude> {{documentation}} <!-- Categories go on the /doc subpage, and interwikis go on Wikidata. --> </noinclude>

revision.comment: updated the instruction regarding documentation and interwikis
revision.text: {{#invoke:Message box|mbox}}<noinclude> {{documentation}} <!-- Add categories to the /doc subpage; interwikis go to Wikidata, thank you! --></noinclude> </noinclude>
{{#invoke:Message box|mbox}}<noinclude> {{documentation}} <!-- Add categories to the /doc subpage; interwikis go to Wikidata, thank you! --> </noinclude>

revision.text: {{#invoke:Message box|mbox}}<noinclude> {{documentation}} <!-- Add categories to the /doc subpage; interwikis go to Wikidata, thank you! --> </noinclude>

revision.comment: Imported from wikipedia at 2025-05-10 19:11:12 on [[Template:Mbox]]
revision.text:
{{{eq|{{{1}}}}}} ---- This template prevents line breaks during HTML mathematics. It is a practical alternative to excessive use of [[non-breaking space]]s ( ). ==Use== In a math article, included math such as 
''f'': ''X'' × ''Y'' → [0, 1]
may end up broken between lines. This can be fixed using nonbreaking spaces: 
''f'': ''X'' × ''Y'' → [0, 1]
However, this results in cumbersome source code which can be difficult to edit. Using this template, the same result can be achieved with: 
{{mbox| ''f'': ''X'' × ''Y'' → [0, 1] }}
===Dealing with equations=== Because of Wikipedia limitations on templates, mbox doesn't function as stated above if the math contains an equals sign. In this case, the following syntax should be used: 
{{mbox|eq= 2''x'' + 3''y'' + 5''z'' = 7 }}

This longer syntax also works for non-equations.

revision.comment: steal unused template for a new high-level meta-template (for interaction between {{ambox}}, {{imbox}} and {{cmbox}}
more rigorous
add {{dmbox}}
add doc
Changed the "#ifeq:" statements to "#if:", changed to using {{namespace detect}} so a demospace parameter can be used and talk space can be easily detected, and other fixes.
template moved
Updating the code comment since ambox and dmbox/ombox has been fixed. They can take empty parameters now.
A first beta version of {{tmbox}} is available, so enabled call to tmbox.
The if-statements do not work, have done extensive testing in my userspace. But no worries, all our mboxes now can take empty parameters so lets use that.
Adding "{{pp-template|small=yes}}".
Adding support for the {{tmbox}} small parameters.
Changed "text = {{{text|}}}" to "text = {{{text}}}" so that the empty box displays "{{{text}}}".
Changed deprecated "{{namespace detect|image=}}" to "{{namespace detect|file=}}, due to change of namespace "Image:" to "File:". No change in functionality.
This template has now been checked and tested, it works correctly in the new "Book:" and "Book talk:" namespaces. And a minor update to "{{pp-template}}".

pass name for benefit of ambox

revision.comment: Imported from wikipedia at 2025-05-10 19:22:45 on [[Template:Mbox]]
revision.text:
{{{eq|{{{1}}}}}} ---- This template prevents line breaks during HTML mathematics. It is a practical alternative to excessive use of [[non-breaking space]]s ( ). ==Use== In a math article, included math such as 
''f'': ''X'' × ''Y'' → [0, 1]
may end up broken between lines. This can be fixed using nonbreaking spaces: 
''f'': ''X'' × ''Y'' → [0, 1]
However, this results in cumbersome source code which can be difficult to edit. Using this template, the same result can be achieved with: 
{{mbox| ''f'': ''X'' × ''Y'' → [0, 1] }}
===Dealing with equations=== Because of Wikipedia limitations on templates, mbox doesn't function as stated above if the math contains an equals sign. In this case, the following syntax should be used: 
{{mbox|eq= 2''x'' + 3''y'' + 5''z'' = 7 }}

revision.comment: /* Dealing with equations */
revision.text:
{{{eq|{{{1}}}}}} ---- This template prevents line breaks during HTML mathematics. It is a practical alternative to excessive use of [[non-breaking space]]s ( ). ==Use== In a math article, included math such as 
''f'': ''X'' × ''Y'' → [0, 1]
may end up broken between lines. This can be fixed using nonbreaking spaces: 
''f'': ''X'' × ''Y'' → [0, 1]
However, this results in cumbersome source code which can be difficult to edit. Using this template, the same result can be achieved with: 
{{mbox| ''f'': ''X'' × ''Y'' → [0, 1] }}
===Dealing with equations=== Because of Wikipedia limitations on templates, mbox doesn't function as stated above if the math contains an equals sign. In this case, the following syntax should be used: 
{{mbox|eq= 2''x'' + 3''y'' + 5''z'' = 7 }}
This longer syntax also works for non-equations.

revision.text:
{{{|{{{1}}}}}} ---- This template prevents line breaks during HTML mathematics. It is a practical alternative to excessive use of [[non-breaking space]]s ( ). ==Use== In a math article, included math such as 
''f'': ''X'' × ''Y'' → [0, 1]
may end up broken between lines. This can be fixed using nonbreaking spaces: 
''f'': ''X'' × ''Y'' → [0, 1]
However, this results in cumbersome source code which can be difficult to edit. Using this template, the same result can be achieved with:

Using |= instead of | also works for non-equations.

{{{|{{{1}}}}}} ---- This template prevents line breaks during HTML mathematics. It is a practical alternative to excessive use of [[non-breaking space]]s ( ). ==Use== In a math article, included math such as 
''f'': ''X'' × ''Y'' → [0, 1]
may end up broken between lines. This can be fixed using nonbreaking spaces:

===Dealing with equations=== Because of Wikipedia limitations on templates, mbox doesn't function as stated above if the math contains an equals sign. In this case, the following syntax should be used: 
{{mbox|= 2''x'' + 3''y'' + 5''z'' = 7}}
Using |= instead of | also works for non-equations. [[Category:Mathematics templates|{{PAGENAME}}]]

revision.comment: steal unused template for a new high-level meta-template (for interaction between {{ambox}}, {{imbox}} and {{cmbox}}
more rigorous
add {{dmbox}}
add doc
Changed the "#ifeq:" statements to "#if:", changed to using {{namespace detect}} so a demospace parameter can be used and talk space can be easily detected, and other fixes.
template moved
Updating the code comment since ambox and dmbox/ombox has been fixed. They can take empty parameters now.
A first beta version of {{tmbox}} is available, so enabled call to tmbox.
The if-statements do not work, have done extensive testing in my userspace. But no worries, all our mboxes now can take empty parameters so lets use that.
Adding "{{pp-template|small=yes}}".
Adding support for the {{tmbox}} small parameters.
Changed "text = {{{text|}}}" to "text = {{{text}}}" so that the empty box displays "{{{text}}}".
Changed deprecated "{{namespace detect|image=}}" to "{{namespace detect|file=}}, due to change of namespace "Image:" to "File:". No change in functionality.
This template has now been checked and tested, it works correctly in the new "Book:" and "Book talk:" namespaces. And a minor update to "{{pp-template}}".
redundant
pass subst and date for benefit of ambox
pass through class
pass name for benefit of ambox
switch to Lua version

{{mbox| ''f'': ''X'' × ''Y'' → [0, 1]}}
===Dealing with equations=== Because of Wikipedia limitations on templates, mbox doesn't function as stated above if the math contains an equals sign. In this case, the following syntax should be used: 
{{mbox|eq= 2''x'' + 3''y'' + 5''z'' = 7}}
This longer syntax also works for non-equations.

more rigorous
add {{dmbox}}

A first beta version of {{tmbox}} is available, so enabled call to tmbox.

template moved
Updating the code comment since ambox and dmbox/ombox has been fixed. They can take empty parameters now.
A first beta version of {{tmbox}} is available, so enabled call to tmbox.
The if-statements do not work, have done extensive testing in my userspace. But no worries, all our mboxes now can take empty parameters so lets use that.
Adding "{{pp-template|small=yes}}".
Adding support for the {{tmbox}} small parameters.
Changed "text = {{{text|}}}" to "text = {{{text}}}" so that the empty box displays "{{{text}}}".
Changed deprecated "{{namespace detect|image=}}" to "{{namespace detect|file=}}, due to change of namespace "Image:" to "File:". No change in functionality.
This template has now been checked and tested, it works correctly in the new "Book:" and "Book talk:" namespaces. And a minor update to "{{pp-template}}".
redundant
pass subst and date for benefit of ambox
pass through class
pass name for benefit of ambox
switch to Lua version
revision.text: {{#invoke:Message box|mbox}}<noinclude> {{documentation}} <!-- Categories go on the /doc subpage, and interwikis go on Wikidata. --> </noinclude>

revision.comment: updated the instruction regarding documentation and interwikis
revision.text: {{#invoke:Message box|mbox}}<noinclude> {{documentation}} <!-- Add categories to the /doc subpage; interwikis go to Wikidata, thank you! --></noinclude> </noinclude>
{{#invoke:Message box|mbox}}<noinclude> {{documentation}} <!-- Add categories to the /doc subpage; interwikis go to Wikidata, thank you! --> </noinclude>

revision.comment: Imported from wikipedia at 2025-05-10 21:27:04
revision.text:
{{{eq|{{{1}}}}}} ---- This template prevents line breaks during HTML mathematics. It is a practical alternative to excessive use of [[non-breaking space]]s ( ). ==Use== In a math article, included math such as 
''f'': ''X'' × ''Y'' → [0, 1]
may end up broken between lines. This can be fixed using nonbreaking spaces: 
''f'': ''X'' × ''Y'' → [0, 1]
However, this results in cumbersome source code which can be difficult to edit. Using this template, the same result can be achieved with: 
{{mbox| ''f'': ''X'' × ''Y'' → [0, 1] }}
===Dealing with equations=== Because of Wikipedia limitations on templates, mbox doesn't function as stated above if the math contains an equals sign. In this case, the following syntax should be used: 
{{mbox|eq= 2''x'' + 3''y'' + 5''z'' = 7 }}

revision.comment: /* Dealing with equations */
revision.text:
{{{eq|{{{1}}}}}} ---- This template prevents line breaks during HTML mathematics. It is a practical alternative to excessive use of [[non-breaking space]]s ( ). ==Use== In a math article, included math such as

However, this results in cumbersome source code which can be difficult to edit. Using this template, the same result can be achieved with: 
{{mbox| ''f'': ''X'' × ''Y'' → [0, 1] }}

{{mbox|eq= 2''x'' + 3''y'' + 5''z'' = 7 }}
This longer syntax also works for non-equations.

However, this results in cumbersome source code which can be difficult to edit. Using this template, the same result can be achieved with: 
{{mbox| ''f'': ''X'' × ''Y'' → [0, 1]}}
===Dealing with equations=== Because of Wikipedia limitations on templates, mbox doesn't function as stated above if the math contains an equals sign. In this case, the following syntax should be used: 
{{mbox|eq= 2''x'' + 3''y'' + 5''z'' = 7}}
This longer syntax also works for non-equations.

This longer syntax also works for non-equations.

revision.comment: steal unused template for a new high-level meta-template (for interaction between {{ambox}}, {{imbox}} and {{cmbox}}
more rigorous
add {{dmbox}}
add doc
Changed the "#ifeq:" statements to "#if:", changed to using {{namespace detect}} so a demospace parameter can be used and talk space can be easily detected, and other fixes.
template moved
Updating the code comment since ambox and dmbox/ombox has been fixed. They can take empty parameters now.
A first beta version of {{tmbox}} is available, so enabled call to tmbox.
The if-statements do not work, have done extensive testing in my userspace. But no worries, all our mboxes now can take empty parameters so lets use that.
Adding "{{pp-template|small=yes}}".
Adding support for the {{tmbox}} small parameters.
Changed "text = {{{text|}}}" to "text = {{{text}}}" so that the empty box displays "{{{text}}}".
Changed deprecated "{{namespace detect|image=}}" to "{{namespace detect|file=}}, due to change of namespace "Image:" to "File:". No change in functionality.
This template has now been checked and tested, it works correctly in the new "Book:" and "Book talk:" namespaces. And a minor update to "{{pp-template}}".
redundant
pass subst and date for benefit of ambox
pass through class
pass name for benefit of ambox
switch to Lua version
